intro: Corymbia pocillum, also known as Red Gum or Red Ironbark, is a species of tree native to eastern Australia, belonging to the myrtle family (Myrtaceae). The health benefits of Corymbia pocillum are attributed to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and wounds. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Corymbia pocillum is commonly used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and extracts, with the most popular preparations including a tea to aid in respiratory health and a tincture to support wound healing.
